task_completion.description: Verify the evidence_logger directory and the four required files are created in the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer, terminal, or IDE file tree showing the folder 'evidence_logger' inside 'C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\' containing 'evidence_logger.talon', 'evidence_actions.talon-list', 'evidence_logger.py', and 'evidence_mode.talon'.

task_completion.description: Verify evidence_logger.talon enables the correct tag and defines the required voice commands.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'evidence_logger.talon' containing 'tag(): user.evidence_logging' and the 9 specific voice commands (e.g., 'evidence new case <user.text>', 'evidence log item <user.text>', 'evidence commit entry', etc.).

task_completion.description: Verify evidence_actions.talon-list defines the correct list and the 10 forensic actions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'evidence_actions.talon-list' with the declaration 'list: user.evidence_action' followed by exactly the 10 required actions (received, transferred, stored, retrieved, examined, photographed, returned, disposed, sealed, unsealed).

task_completion.description: Verify evidence_logger.py contains the correct CSV file path and headers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'evidence_logger.py' with the file path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\chain_of_custody.csv' and the exact headers: CaseID, Timestamp, EvidenceItem, Handler, Action, Location, EntryNumber.

task_completion.description: Verify evidence_logger.py implements ISO 8601 timestamps and auto-incrementing entry numbers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'evidence_logger.py' containing Python logic for generating ISO 8601 timestamps (e.g., using datetime.now().isoformat()) and logic to auto-increment the EntryNumber for each new log entry.

task_completion.description: Verify evidence_mode.talon contains the window title condition, tag enablement, and global command.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing 'evidence_mode.talon' with a context header matching the window title 'Evidence Logger' (e.g., 'os: windows\nwin.title: /Evidence Logger/'), enabling 'user.evidence_logging', and defining the global command 'evidence mode on'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually created and edited the files in the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the files open in a text editor (like VS Code, Notepad, or Vim) or terminal output confirming the file contents were written to the correct directory, rather than just printing the code to the chat.

integrity.description: Verify the Python script contains logic to write to the CSV, not just a hardcoded mock CSV output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python file should contain standard file I/O operations (like 'with open(...) as f:' or 'csv.writer') rather than just a hardcoded multi-line string of a finished CSV.

integrity.description: Verify the Talon files use actual Talon syntax.
integrity.visual_evidence: The .talon files should show proper command-to-action mapping syntax (e.g., 'command: user.action()') and not just plain text notes or Python code inside the .talon files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target directory for files: C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\Talon\user\evidence_logger. CSV log path: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\chain_of_custody.csv. The agent must create exactly four files: evidence_logger.talon, evidence_actions.talon-list, evidence_logger.py, and evidence_mode.talon with the specified contents.
